publicSideBand: No direct charge to the customer when Denver Water replaces the line under the Lead Reduction Program or during water main work
privateSideBand: $3800 one-time reimbursement for approved off-schedule replacements and the owner covers the remaining private-side cost
fullReplacementBand: No direct charge under the main program but owner-managed early replacement uses the $3800 reimbursement path
housingTypeAssumption: Customer-owned lead service line in Denver Water's service area replaced through the utility program or approved reimbursement flow
permitAssumption: Reimbursement cases must be approved in advance and cannot conflict with paving moratoriums
restorationAssumption: Owner-managed replacement scope varies when using the reimbursement option
methodologySummary: Denver does not publish a local homeowner bid range for early replacement and instead splits cost between the no-direct-charge utility-run Lead Reduction Program and a capped $3800 reimbursement path for approved off-schedule cases.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners avoid direct cost only when Denver Water schedules the address in the utility program or replaces the line during water-main work and otherwise move into an owner-managed contractor path with capped reimbursement.
